LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS YONG CHANG (AKA YONG AARON CHANG), AND MAY PANG'S (AKA YAMEI PANG) MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OTHERWISE DENIED BECAUSE THE COURT CANNOT NOW DETERMINE THAT THE COMMUNICATIONS HAVE NO LEGITIMATE EVIDENTIARY ROLE. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS YONG CHANG (AKA YONG AARON CHANG, AND MAY PANG'S (AKA YAMEI PANG) SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE FIRST, THIRD, AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF THE PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (CCP 425.16) IS DENIED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 3, GRANTED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 7. DEFENDANTS HAVE CARRIED THEIR BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE CHALLENGED CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE AT LEAST IN PART FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. PLAINTIFFS HAVE SHOWN A SUFFICIENT LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 3 BASED IN PART ON EVIDENCE THAT COULD SUPPORT THE CONCLUSION THAT DEFENDANTS DID NOT INTEND TO PURSUE LITIGATION. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PRESENTED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SHOWING EITHER OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT OR SUFFICIENT EMOTIONAL DAMAGE TO SUPPORT INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. JEROD HENDRICKSON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF LAWRENCE M. SCANCARELLI, TEL. NO. 398-1644 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JETHRO S. BUSCH OF THE LAW OFFICES OF STEVEN ADAIR MACDONALD & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 956-6488 X29 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS YONG CHANG AKA YONG AARON CHANG AND MAY PANG AKA YAMEI PANG'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to cause of action 9, Otherwise overruled. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol A. Karen CSR# 8189 = (301/PJB/AY)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. JEROD HENDRICKSON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF LAWRENCE M. SCANCARELLI, TEL. NO. 398-1644 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JETHRO S. BUSCH OF THE LAW OFFICES OF STEVEN ADAIR MACDONALD & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 956-6488 X29 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS YONG CHANG AKA YONG AARON CHANG AND MAY PANG AKA YAMEI PANGS' MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted as to punitive damages, Otherwise denied because the court cannot now determine that the communications have no legitimate evidentiary role. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol A. Karen CSR# 8189 = (301/PJB/AY)

Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike The First, Third, And Seventh Causes Of Action Of The Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint; Declarations Of Yong Chan And May Pang In Support
Off Calendar Feb-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. JEROD HENDRICKSON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF LAWRENCE M. SCANCARELLI, TEL. NO. 398-1644 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JETHRO S. BUSCH OF THE LAW OFFICES OF STEVEN ADAIR MACDONALD & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 956-6488 X29 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS YONG CHANG AKA YONG AARON CHANG AND MAY PANG AKA YAMEI PANG'S Special Motion To Strike The First, Third, And Seventh Causes Of Action Of The Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint (CCP 425.16) is denied as to causes of action 1 and 3, granted as to cause of action 7. Defendants have carried their burden of showing that the challenged causes of action arise at least in part from protected activity. Plaintiffs have shown a sufficient likelihood of success as to causes of action 1 and 3 based in part on evidence that could support the conclusion that defendants did not intend to pursue litigation. Plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence showing either outrageous conduct or sufficient emotional damage to support Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol A. Karen CSR# 8189 = (301/PJB/AY)
